questions.question: How do AI, machine learning, and generative AI relate?

answers.text: Generative AI is a subset of deep learning, which is a subset of ML, which is a subset of AI
AI is a subset of machine learning

questions.feedback: They nest: AI ⊃ machine learning ⊃ deep learning ⊃ generative AI.

questions.question: A bank wants to predict whether each transaction is fraudulent or legitimate, using historical transactions already labelled as fraud/not-fraud. What kind of ML is this?
questions.feedback: Labelled examples with known categories (fraud / not fraud) make it supervised classification.

questions.question: In the ML lifecycle, which stage is typically the most time-consuming and most affects final model quality?
questions.feedback: Data collection and preparation dominate the effort, and 'garbage in, garbage out' means data quality caps model quality.

questions.question: When is a pre-trained AWS AI service (rather than a custom SageMaker model) the better choice?

answers.text: The need is a common task like transcription, translation, or sentiment analysis
You want to avoid collecting training data and managing the ML lifecycle
The problem is highly specific to your business with no matching pre-built service
You want the lowest effort solution that meets the need

questions.feedback: Pre-trained services fit common tasks with minimal effort. A highly specific problem with no matching service is where custom SageMaker work is justified.
